title: Personal Security / Intrusión
steps: If you suspect an intruder, do not announce your presence.
Lock yourself in a room with a phone and call 112/911.
Turn off lights and stay silent.
If confronted, try to escape to a safe location outside.
Provide a description of the intruder to the operator if possible.
Stay on the line until police arrive.

title: Active Shooter (Run, Hide, Fight)
steps: RUN: Have an escape route and plan in mind. Leave your belongings. Keep your hands visible.
HIDE: Hide in an area out of the shooter's view. Block entry to your hiding place and lock doors. Silence your cell phone.
FIGHT: As a last resort and only when your life is in imminent danger. Attempt to incapacitate the shooter. Act with physical aggression.
Call 112/911 only when it is safe to do so.
When law enforcement arrives, keep your hands empty and visible at all times. Follow all instructions.

title: Suspicious Package / Bomb Threat
steps: Do NOT touch, move, or open the package.
Evacuate the immediate area and prevent others from entering.
Do NOT use two-way radios or cell phones within 50 feet (15 meters) of the object.
Call emergency services from a safe distance outside the building.
Be prepared to provide a description: size, shape, markings, and any sounds (ticking) or smells.
Follow evacuation routes and stay away from windows or glass partitions.

title: Abduction / Kidnapping Attempt
steps: If someone attempts to force you into a vehicle, fight back aggressively and yell for help.
Draw as much attention as possible to the situation.
If you are abducted, try to leave markers (scent, clothing, objects) for search teams.
Note descriptions of the abductor: age, height, marks/tattoos, and vehicle details.
If safe, use your phone to send your 'Live Location' to trust contacts immediately.
Stay as calm as possible and look for any opportunity to escape safely.

title: Animal Attack (Domestic / Wild)
steps: DOG: Stay still, do not make eye contact. If it attacks, use a jacket or bag as a shield.
BEAR: Speak calmly and back away slowly without turning your back. If it's a grizzly, play dead; if black bear, fight back.
SNAKE: Back away slowly; do not attempt to kill it. If bitten, stay calm and keep the limb below heart level.
WILD CAT (Cougar/Lion): Stand tall, look large, and do NOT run (triggers chase instinct).
Seek immediate medical attention for any bite or scratch (risk of rabies/infection).

title: Identity Theft / Digital Security Breach
steps: If you suspect identity theft, freeze your credit reports immediately with major bureaus.
Change all passwords for sensitive accounts (banking, email, social media) using a safe device.
Enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on all accounts that support it.
Notify your bank and credit card issuers to flag fraudulent transactions.
File a report with the police or your national identity theft agency.
Monitor your financial statements and credit reports closely for at least 12 months.

title: Civil Unrest / Riot Survival
steps: If you see a large, agitated crowd forming, leave the area immediately.
Avoid wearing clothes that make you look like a participant or a specific target.
If caught in a crowd, stay on the periphery. Do not move against the flow of people.
Avoid glass storefronts and entranceways; stay close to sturdy walls.
If you are indoors, stay away from windows and lock all doors. Turn off lights if the building is a target.
Do not stop to watch or take photos; your priority is moving to a safe, quiet location.

title: Home Invasion (Occupied)
steps: If someone breaks in while you are home, your priority is escape. Use a window or back door if possible.
If you cannot escape, LOCK yourself in a 'safe room' (like a bedroom or bathroom) and barricade the door.
Call emergency services immediately and stay on the line. Speak in a whisper.
If confronted, do not resist if the intruder only wants property. Give them what they ask for.
Avoid making eye contact or sudden movements that could be interpreted as a threat.
Try to remember specific details about the intruder for the police report.

title: Vehicle Carjacking
steps: If someone attempts to take your car by force, do NOT resist. Your life is more valuable than the vehicle.
Get yourself and any passengers out of the car as quickly as possible.
Keep your hands visible and avoid making sudden movements.
If there is a child in the car, state clearly: 'My child is in the car, I am taking them out.'
Once you are out, move away from the vehicle and call emergency services immediately.
Provide a description of the car, license plate, and the direction in which the thief fled.

title: Stalking / Persistent Harassment
steps: Trust your instincts. If you feel you are being followed, go to a well-lit, public place immediately.
Do NOT go home if you are being followed. Drive to a police station or fire station.
Keep a detailed log of all incidents, including dates, times, locations, and descriptions.
Inform family, friends, and coworkers about the situation so they can watch for suspicious activity.
Enhance your home security (cameras, locks) and vary your daily routes and routines.
Seek a protection order or restraining order through local law enforcement.

title: ATM / Cash Withdrawal Security
steps: Use well-lit, busy ATMs. Avoid using them late at night or in isolated areas.
Check the ATM for skimmers or loose parts before inserting your card.
Shield the keypad with your hand while entering your PIN.
If you are approached by someone after a withdrawal, do not count your money; put it away and leave immediately.
If someone attempts to rob you, comply. Your cash is replaceable, your life is not.
Call the bank and the police immediately if your card is stolen or you suspect fraudulent activity.

title: Workplace Violence / Suspicious Colleague
steps: If a colleague makes direct or indirect threats, report it to HR or Security immediately. Do NOT ignore it.
Watch for 'Red Flags': extreme agitation, mentions of weapons, or sudden changes in behavior.
If an incident occurs, move to a safe room with a lock and stay quiet.
Avoid escalating the situation by arguing or confronting the aggressive individual alone.
Familiarize yourself with your company's emergency response plan and exit routes.
Call emergency services if you believe there is an immediate threat to life or safety.
